description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Fe–10Mn–10Al–0.7C low-density steel specimens, including the hot-rolled (HR) sample and samples annealed at 700°C, 800°C, 850°C, 900°C, 950°C, 1000°C, 1100°C, 1200°C for 1 hour respectively. The x-axis is 2θ ranging from 30° to 90°, and the y-axis is diffraction intensity in counts per second (cps). Identified phases from the peaks are α-ferrite, γ-austenite, and κ-carbide. Obvious κ-carbide peaks are detected in samples annealed at 700°C, 800°C and 850°C, while no distinct γ phase peaks appear in the 700°C annealed sample. No κ-carbide peaks are observed in the hot-rolled sample, attributed to its low volume fraction or coherency with the α matrix. For samples annealed at 900°C and above, κ-carbide peaks vanish, only α and γ phase peaks remain. This figure confirms that annealing temperature significantly modulates the phase constitution of the steel, and the dissolution temperature of κ-carbide falls in the range of 850°C to 900°C.
